content.full_text:
WARREN SLOCUM
COUNTY CLERK-RECORDER

FPPC

AUG 2 0 1992

SAN MATEO COUNTY

Hall of Justice and Records • 401 Marshall Street
Redwood City • California 94063-1636 • (415) 363-4711
FAX: (415) 363-4914

August 18, 1992

Sandra Taylor, Political Reform Consultant
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Ms. Taylor:

Regarding: Consolidation request of Heritage Site Plan
Initiative, I.D. #921390

We received the enclosed request from the Heritage Site Plan
Initiative to consolidate its reporting requirements pursuant to
Government Code Section 84205. We are forwarding it to your
Commission for review. Please notify us if the consolidation
request has been approved.

Sincerely,

Warren Slocum
County Clerk-Recorder

Enclosure:
WS:st

cc: Peter A. Bagatelos

Officium Populi—Office of the People.

Clerk Division                          Recorder Division                    Registration-Election Division
Constance Carter                       Terence Medina                       Maureen Grover
Assistant Clerk-Recorder              Deputy Clerk-Recorder                Deputy Clerk-Recorder
(415) 363-4711                        (415) 363-4713                       (415) 312-5222

LAW OFFICES OF

BAGATELOS & FADEM
THE INTERNATIONAL BUILDING
601 CALIFORNIA STREET
SUITE 1801
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108

BARRY FADEM
PETER A. BAGATELOS

TELEPHONE
(415) 982-7100
FAX
(415) 982-1085

August 3, 1992

Ms. Sharon Theophiles
San Mateo County Clerk--Recorders Office
Hall of Justice and Records
401 Marshall Street, Room A
Redwood City, California 94063-1636

Re: Heritage Site Plan Initiative, Sponsored by Half Moon Bay Heritage Company, Ltd.
I.D. #921390

Dear Ms. Theophiles:

This firm represents the Heritage Site Plan Initiative, sponsored by Half Moon Bay
Heritage Company, Ltd. The committee is registered with the Secretary of State's office and
is required to file campaign reports with your office. This will confirm my telephone
conversation with you on July 31, 1992 regarding our request that our client be able to
consolidate its reporting obligations pursuant to Government Code §84205. This is explained
more below.

The committee recently filed its semi-annual report for the period covering from
January 1 through June 30, 1992. The committee is required to file pre-election reports, with
respect to a November 3, 1992 election date, on October 5 (for the period July 1 through
September 30, 1992), and on October 22 (for the period October 1 through October 17, 1992).

The committee is also required, according to recent changes in the Political Reform Act,
to file a campaign statement 21 days after the maximum deadline for filing petitions. This date
is October 7, 1992, which is 180 days after April 10, 1992, the date on which the initiative title
and summary was received by the committee. Therefore, a statement will have to be filed on
October 28, 1992 (for the period October 18 through October 21, 1992).

Thus, you can see that the committee will be required to file a report on October 22, as
well as anther report six days later that has a four day period of coverage. This is burdensome
on the committee and is the type of situation contemplated by Government Code §84205, which
allows committees to obtain special permission to consolidate closely related reports. Since filing on October 28 is approximately one week before the November 3 election, disclosure on
October 28 of all information through October 21, 1992 would be very timely with respect to
the election. Accordingly, we ask on behalf of our client for permission under Government
Code §84205 to allow our client to file one report that encompasses both the second pre-election
report and the 21 day post report.

Ms. Sharon Theophiles
August 3, 1992
Page 2

We were advised by the Fair Political Practices Commission staff to notify your office
as the local filing officer for this permission. Under the Government Code, the Commission
must approve this consolidated filing by regulation or written advice. However, where a local
jurisdiction is involved, the FPPC apparently prefers the local official to communicate the request
to the Commission, rather than the filer. I was advised by Sandra Taylor at the FPPC's
Technical Assistant's office to follow this procedure. Enclosed herewith are two recent advice
letters to Diane Fishburn and Kay Woodson, in which the Commission has expressed its views
regarding consolidation. Based on these letters, it seems fairly clear that our request should be
granted, on the grounds that the consolidation will not result in a significant extension of the
deadline for filing a particular statement, a very short time period is involved, and consolidation
will not interfere with timely public access to disclosure information.

Thank you very much for your cooperation. Please advise at the earliest opportunity as
to whether our request is granted.

Very truly yours,

Peter A. Bagatelos
Peter A. Bagatelos
PAB:bz

Enclosures

cc: Edward Gee, Treasurer
